module_map.responsibility: Orchestration layer that drives the entire pipeline: initialize flags/config, fetch metadata for all paths, apply sorting, and invoke display
Collect file metadata: permissions, ownership, size, timestamps, file type, symlinks, git status. Struct Meta holds all per-file attributes
CLI and config file flag parsing; define output columns (Block enum), sorting/filtering modes, display layout
submodules: flags/blocks.rs (Block enum: Name, Size, Date, Permission, Owner, etc.; Blocks collection)

module_map.responsibility: Format and render files to stdout; applies padding rules, tree prefixes, colors, icons; converts Meta + Block columns to text output
Assemble sort comparators from Flags.sorting; applies sort order to Vec<Meta>
Color scheme resolution (Git-aware, LSColors, custom themes); map file attributes (type, permissions, git status) to ANSI colors
Icon theme resolution; map file name/type to Unicode or Nerd Font icons

data_flow.description: Parse CLI args + config file into Flags struct; initialize Colors, Icons, GitTheme from flags
For each input path, collect: file type, permissions, owner, size, timestamps, symlink target, git status. Recursively fetch children if directory.
types_produced: Meta (contains: name, size, date, permissions, owner, file_type, symlink, git_status)

data_flow.description: Build sort comparator chain from Flags.sorting (by name, size, date, etc.). Apply to metas Vec in-place.

data_flow.key_function: get_output(meta, owner_cache, colors, icons, git_theme, flags, ...) -> String
data_flow.description: For each Meta, iterate over Blocks (columns). Render each Block field (name, size, date, etc.) using: Colors for ANSI escapes, Icons for icon theme, Permissions for rwx text.
types_produced: String (one line of output per file)

data_flow.description: Collect rendered strings; calculate max width per column; apply padding rules (HashMap<Block, usize>). For tree layout, prepend tree structure (├──, etc.).
types_produced: String (formatted output with padding and tree prefixes)

data_flow.description: Print final formatted strings to stdout

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central data struct (Meta) consumed by display, sort, core. Imports from permissions, owner, size, date, filetype, symlink, name, access_control, git_file_status, windows_* modules for field construction.
Sole renderer; imports Meta, Flags, Block types, Colors, Icons, GitTheme, DisplayOption, and uses sort module. Formats and outputs all data.
Block enum is widely used (display, sort, padding calculations). Parsing logic for Block from CLI and config affects core initialization.

change_proposal.objective: Add SHA-256 file checksum display column to lsd
files_to_modify: src/flags/blocks.rs -- Add Checksum variant to Block enum; update Block::get_header(); update parsing logic (from_cli, from_config, try_from)
src/meta/mod.rs -- Add checksum field to Meta struct; compute SHA-256 in Meta::from_path() for regular files (skip dirs/symlinks)
src/display.rs -- In get_output(), handle Block::Checksum branch: render meta.checksum as hex string, apply truncation if needed
src/flags/flags.rs (or similar config) -- Update Blocks default/initialization to include/exclude Checksum based on config
src/theme/color.rs -- Optionally: add ThemeColor variant for checksum highlighting (e.g., dim gray for hashes)
new_types: Meta.checksum: Option<String> (SHA-256 in hex; None for directories/symlinks)
Optional: ChecksumDisplay enum (Full hash, first 8 chars, last 8 chars)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Similar to Block::Size pattern: (1) Add enum variant in Block; (2) Add field in Meta; (3) Compute in from_path() using std::fs::read() + sha2 crate; (4) Render in get_output() with color from Colors; (5) Add CLI flag via flags/blocks.rs; (6) Support enable/disable in config_file.rs
change_proposal.integration_point: Meta::from_path() reads file contents for hash (I/O cost). get_output() handles rendering in the Block column iterator. Flags::checksum_length or ChecksumDisplay enum controls truncation. GitTheme irrelevant; use Colors::file for styling.
risks: I/O overhead: SHA-256 on every file; consider lazy evaluation or caching for large directories. Option: only hash on demand (e.g., --checksum flag) or skip directories.
Symlink handling: Should checksum follow symlinks? Recommend: skip (hash=None) unless --follow-symlinks. Test edge cases.
Performance regression: Large files cause slow hashing. Mitigation: add --no-checksum flag; parallelization via rayon (already used for filetype detection).
Portability: sha2 crate is Rust std; cross-platform. No risk.
Display width: Long hash strings (64 chars) exceed typical terminal width. Mitigation: Config option for truncation (first N chars, last N chars, or digest shorthand).

tool_usage.purpose: Identify top-level modules and file counts
Understand Meta struct and from_path() function signature
Understand display pipeline and get_output() rendering
Understand Block enum and column configuration
Trace callers and callees of metadata collection entry point
Trace rendering pipeline for each file output
Trace top-level data flow from core::fetch
Understand sorting stage between metadata collection and display

summary.codebase_structure: lsd is a Rust ls clone organized in layers: (1) core orchestrates; (2) meta collects file attributes into Meta struct; (3) flags parses CLI/config into Block enum and other options; (4) sort applies ordering; (5) display renders via get_output() which iterates Block columns; (6) theme/color/icon provide styling. Total: 52 files, 13k LOC, 87 types.
summary.key_insight: Data flows as Vec<Meta> through the pipeline. Each Meta is a snapshot of file attributes (permissions, owner, size, date, git_status, etc.). Display is column-oriented: Block enum defines which columns to show; get_output() renders each column by delegating to field-specific render functions (render_permission, render_owner, render_size, etc.). Adding checksum requires: (1) Meta.checksum field + SHA-256 computation in from_path(); (2) Block::Checksum variant; (3) render branch in get_output().
summary.architectural_strengths: Modular column system (Block enum); lazy evaluation of attributes (each Meta field computed only if needed); theme/color abstraction; config file + CLI flag parsing unified in flags/
summary.change_complexity: Low-to-medium. Block enum extension is straightforward; from_path() addition involves I/O (can be parallelized); get_output() renderer is formulaic (similar to Size, Date blocks). Risk: performance if hashing all files without opt-out.
